title: @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload and Resource Budgets · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible lib-storage multipart adoption contract with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in lib-storage multipart adoption contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Pin @aws-sdk/lib-storage 3.1087.0 with its peer S3 client line and wrap Upload behind characterized queueSize, partSize, leavePartsOnError, progress, abort, cleanup, and result contracts.
Inventory v2 ManagedUpload and hand-written multipart behavior, map queue, part, progress, abort, cleanup, and result differences, then migrate to v3 Upload behind contract tests.
Choose PutObjectCommand or lib-storage Upload from body size and replayability, and validate Node Readable, Web stream, Blob, progress-event, checksum, and completion ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Transfer Resource Budget
steps.instruction:
Order the Transfer Resource Budget S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript storage-delivery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Transfer Resource Budget.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Transfer Resource Budget assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Transfer Resource Budget, keep Transfer Resource Budget connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5960 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload workers, part buffers, abort ownership, and cleanup policy; keep AbortSignal and stream cleanup under one named owner; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound aggregate Upload queueSize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, AbortControllers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Transfer Resource Budget has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound aggregate Upload queueSize workers, part buffers, active source streams, sockets, progress listeners, AbortControllers, descriptors, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Transfer Resource Budget, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: queueSize limits the entire Node process rather than multiplying workers and buffered parts for every active Upload instance.
